prompt: I'm working on the KaMPKit KMP project (github.com/touchlab/KaMPKit) and want to upgrade to AGP 9.0. We're currently on AGP 8.11.2. The shared module applies both kotlin.multiplatform and com.android.library. I keep getting build errors about incompatibility. The project files are provided. Can you migrate this project to AGP 9.0?
source: Real project: touchlab/KaMPKit — shared has KMP+library (Path A), app has kotlin.android
agent_rubric: The shared module's com.android.library plugin is replaced with com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library (or a version catalog alias for it)
The shared module's android {} configuration block is nested inside kotlin { } (e.g. kotlin { androidLibrary { } } or kotlin { android { } }), not at the top level
Source set androidUnitTest is renamed to androidHostTest in the shared module
The org.jetbrains.kotlin.android plugin is removed from the app module (AGP 9.0 has built-in Kotlin support)
AGP version in gradle/libs.versions.toml is updated to 9.0.0 or higher
Gradle wrapper in gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties is updated to 9.1.0 or higher
The SKIE plugin (co.touchlab.skie) compatibility with AGP 9 is handled — either updated to a compatible version, flagged with an opt-out property, or documented as a known issue
No removed gradle properties are present (android.enableLegacyVariantApi, android.r8.integratedResourceShrinking, android.enableNewResourceShrinker.preciseShrinking)
The version catalog (libs.versions.toml) has a plugin alias for the new com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library plugin
The migration does not break the existing dependency structure between app and shared modules
